programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: With wildfire season ahead, Mike works with an aerial firefighting company as they fix and convert aircraft into firefighting machines, including the transformation of a 1960's C130 plane from aged military aircraft into a stateofthe art waterbomber.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: The engineering secrets behind a mobile research station in Antarctica, a gravity-defying concert hall on the Canary Islands, and a rotating boat lift in Scotland.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Visiting the Hearst Tower in Manhattan and Jewel Changi Airport in Singapore to understand how they were built; covering how designers and engineers attempt to create a museum worthy of Salvador Dali in Florida.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Traveling from Canada to Belgium to the Southern U.S. to examine an eye-popping school, museum and railway station and reveal how they were built.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Uncovering the design and engineering secrets behind the Grand Canyon Skywalk, the Audain Museum and more.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: The inside stories behind a new, horseshoe-shaped complex in Rotterdam, a rock-shaped opera house in China and a makeover of the British Museum.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: Experts examine how the Chernobyl nuclear disaster and the Bhopal gas tragedy happened; they also explore the consequences and how they could have been avoided.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When an A300 stalls and crashes just before landing in Japan, fears arise about the plane's complex automated systems.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: On 26 May 1991, the thrust reverser on the number one engine of Lauda Air Flight 004 deploys in flight without being commanded, causing the aircraft to spiral out of control, disintegrate, and crash about 100 miles northwest of Bangkok.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: On August 22nd, 1985, 55 people died aboard British Airtours flight 28M when its engine catches fire as it accelerates.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Just after takeoff from Honolulu, TransAir Flight 810 loses power and ditches in the Pacific. Both pilots survive, blaming dual engine failure-but when the Boeing 737 is recovered, the wreckage reveals a different cause behind t.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Investigators struggle to understand the unusual flight path and Himalayan crash site of Thai Airways Flight 311.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A helicopter carrying oil workers to an offshore drilling platform crashes.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Mistaken identity and hostile fire over enemy territory are among the many risks that pilots face in war zones around the world.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: From corrosion and metal fatigue to faulty repairs, three doomed airlines are brought down by gradually deteriorating parts.

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: It is considered the largest sea battle in history: the Battle of Leyte Gulf in WWII was three days of ingenious strategy and white-knuckle combat between warships, aircraft and kamikazes.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Stories of espionage at sea, including deadly U-boat cat-and-mouse games during WWI, the sinking of the Lusitania, a gun-smuggling private yacht that made history, and liners turned troopships.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The legendary Battle of Trafalgar in 1805, which pitted Napoleon's French ships against a British armada under the command of Admiral Horatio Nelson.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A portrayal of a crucial September 1950 military operation that helped the UN and ROK gain the upper hand over North Korea in the Korean War.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Examining some of maritime history's most effective lightning strikes; how the French frigate Nivôse set a trap to lure Somali pirates; how the HMS Illustrious captured a well-defended Mediterranean harbor in WWII; a surprise attack on Tokyo in 1942.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at how the art of deception was used by naval forces in the first half of the 20th century; in 1939, the German raider Atlantis is equipped with fake funnels, while its sailors dressed as women pushing prams to fool Allied merchant ships.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Revolutionary ship design helped Sir John Hawkins and Sir Francis Drake lead England to defeat the Spanish Armada and become a world power.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at Naval warfare's fastest combat ships and how the advantage of speed determined the outcome of some of the great seas battles of the last 150 years.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Bad design, human error and the enemy sent great ships to tragic ends, like Britain's K13 submarine and HMS Hood, Sweden's 17th century Vasa, and Kublai Khan's fleet, sunk by the original kamikaze.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The evolution from wooden ships to metal ones in the second half of the 19th century is examined.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The history of the B-1 Lancer reveals how the Cold War bomber evolved to become one of Americas most effective military aircraft.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: A countdown of the most influential and impactful military planes of the Cold War, from America's B-2 to the Soviet's MiG-15.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The inside story of the F-86 Sabre, one of America's first fighter jets, and its epic battles against the Soviet MiG jet during the Korean War and beyond.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: A look at the top U.S. military aircraft in America's modern arsenal, including the F-117 Nighthawk stealth fighter, the hybrid V-22 Osprey, and more.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The rapid evolution of drones and how they're changing the future of aviation.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The inside story of the ultimate stealth fighter, the F-22 Raptor, and its predecessors.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: Exploring the world's top close air support aircraft, which enter battles at a minute's notice.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: The history of the KC-135 Stratotanker, the 60-year-old aerial refueler that keeps fighter jets flying.

programme.desc: The North American F-100 Super Saber, also known as the Hun, becomes a supersonic powerhouse after initial difficulties; it was used primarily in the Vietnam War.

programme.desc: The 35-year history of the UH-60 Black Hawk.

programme.desc: A look at how the C-47 evolved from passenger plane to a transport aircraft and flying hospital in WWII and years later as a gunship in Vietnam.

programme.title: Air Warriors
programme.desc: Stories of fighter aircraft and deadly combat, including dogfights with the P-38 Lightning and Messerschmitts over Nazi Germany, told through rarely seen battle footage and first-hand accounts.

programme.desc: The story of the world's first nuclear bomber, the B-29, and its explosive impact on aerial warfare.

programme.desc: An inside look at Japan's A6M Zero, the most feared fighter at the start of World War II.

programme.title: Aerial America
programme.desc: A coast-to-coast aerial tour celebrates the best small towns in the United States and tells stories about the men and women who called them home.

programme.desc: The South's famous landmarks, historical milestones and unique culture.

programme.desc: An aerial tour of Florida's peninsula and panhandle celebrates the state's sights, unique history and lavish lifestyles.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When a USAF stealth bomber crashes after takeoff, investigators search for a reason.
